text.title: spatial-ui-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for spatial-ui-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Head-locked primary navigation — a persistent menu that follows gaze at all times.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Causes visual fatigue within minutes; blocks the view of world content; violates Apple visionOS and Meta Horizon OS design guidelines explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Placing any UI closer than 0.5 m from the user's eye point.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Content within 0.5 m is in the vergence-accommodation conflict zone; eyes cannot focus comfortably and the user experiences eye strain within seconds.

failure-mode.description: Requiring constant arm raising to interact with world-locked UI above eye level.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gorilla arm fatigue sets in after 30-60 seconds of raised-arm interaction; UI above the horizontal midline must be transient or reachable by gaze+pinch, not by reaching up.
